title: OS X/clang C++ flags in the generated makefile
body:
Hello, 

I'm using Verilator on OS X. I'm not sure if the issue described here is intentional or more of a user error.
Verilator version
Verilator 4.035
compiled with
Apple clang version 11.0.3 (clang-1103.0.32.59)
Verilator configures and builds itself with
`-std=gnu++14`
however, this option does not propagate to the generated Makefiles. This breaks e.g. --trace runs as shown below. Of course passing the proper CFLAGS to verilator, or adding them manually to the Makefile fixes the error. Would it be more appropriate to bake in the same -std value into the generated makefiles, given that stuff will get compiled together at source level?

```
In file included from /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_vcd_c.h:24:
In file included from /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_trace.h:27:
In file included from /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/usr/bin/../include/c++/v1/string:504:
In file included from /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/usr/bin/../include/c++/v1/string_view:175:
In file included from /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/usr/bin/../include/c++/v1/__string:56:
In file included from /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/usr/bin/../include/c++/v1/algorithm:643:
In file included from /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/usr/bin/../include/c++/v1/memory:656:
/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/usr/bin/../include/c++/v1/new:163:6: error: enumeration previously
      declared as scoped
enum align_val_t { __zero = 0, __max = (size_t)-1 };
     ^
note: previous declaration is here
/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/usr/bin/../include/c++/v1/new:203:102: error: no type named
      'align_val_t' in namespace 'std'
  ..._LIBCPP_OVERRIDABLE_FUNC_VIS void* operator new(std::size_t __sz, std::align_val_t) _THROW_BAD_A...
                                                                       ~~~~~^
```
code_blocks:

note: previous declaration is here

  ..._LIBCPP_OVERRIDABLE_FUNC_VIS void* operator new(std::size_t __sz, std::align_val_t) _THROW_BAD_A...
                                                                       ~~~~~^
